message:
fix(http2): handle unknown extension frame types during body reads

Add FrameType::Unknown to the match arms in all three HTTP/2 frame
dispatch paths (App, Handler, and Closure/TcpServer) so that unknown
extension frames received during body reading are gracefully ignored
instead of causing connection errors. Per RFC 9113 Section 4.1,
implementations MUST ignore and discard frames of unknown types.

Add three integration tests verifying that interleaved unknown
extension frames (type 0xA) between HEADERS and DATA on a POST
stream do not break request processing in any of the server paths:
- http2_app_path_allows_interleaved_unknown_extension_while_reading_body
- http2_handler_path_allows_interleaved_unknown_extension_while_reading_body
- http2_closure_path_allows_interleaved_unknown_extension_while_reading_body

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
